parts.title: SKILL 14: LISTEN FOR WISHES
parts.instruction:
TOEFL EXERCISE 14: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question 
on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should remember 
that a wish implies an opposite meaning.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think we'll be able to get tick ets for the concert?
(woman) I wish there weren't so many people in line in front of us.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The line is short.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states 'I wish there weren't so many people in line,' which means the opposite is true: the line is long.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya berharap tidak ada begitu banyak orang dalam antrean,' yang berarti kebalikannya yang benar: antrean itu panjang.

options.text: There are not very many people in front of them.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's statement 'I wish there weren't so many people in line' directly contradicts this option. She wishes for fewer people, implying there are currently many.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan wanita itu 'Saya berharap tidak ada begitu banyak orang dalam antrean' secara langsung bertentangan dengan pilihan ini. Dia berharap ada lebih sedikit orang, menyiratkan bahwa saat ini ada banyak.

options.text: The line in front of them is too long.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: Listen for Wishes and Untrue Conditions, the woman's statement 'I wish there weren't so many people in line' expresses a desire contrary to the current reality. This implies that there are indeed many people in line, making it too long.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: Listen for Wishes and Untrue Conditions, pernyataan wanita itu 'Saya berharap tidak ada begitu banyak orang dalam antrean' mengungkapkan keinginan yang bertentangan dengan kenyataan saat ini. Ini menyiratkan bahwa memang ada banyak orang dalam antrean, membuatnya terlalu panjang.

options.text: Not many people want to get tickets to the concert.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's statement focuses on the length of the line itself, not on the overall demand for concert tickets. The number of people in line doesn't necessarily reflect the total number of people wanting tickets.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan wanita itu berfokus pada panjang antrean itu sendiri, bukan pada permintaan keseluruhan untuk tiket konser. Jumlah orang dalam antrean tidak selalu mencerminkan jumlah total orang yang menginginkan tiket.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes and Untrue Conditions

questions.passage:
(woman) I'm sorry I didn't tell you about the parking ticket.
(man) I wish you had told me about it.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The woman told him about the ticket.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man says 'I wish you had told me,' which implies the opposite happened; he was not told.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu berkata 'Saya harap kamu memberitahuku,' yang menyiratkan kebalikannya yang terjadi; dia tidak diberitahu.

options.text: He wanted the woman to get a ticket.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement is about wishing for information, not about wanting the woman to receive a ticket.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria itu adalah tentang berharap mendapatkan informasi, bukan tentang ingin wanita itu menerima tiket.

options.text: He was happy to find out about the ticket.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'I wish you had told me' expresses regret or a desire for a different past event, indicating he was not happy about finding out indirectly or not at all.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'Saya harap kamu memberitahuku' mengungkapkan penyesalan atau keinginan untuk kejadian masa lalu yang berbeda, yang menunjukkan bahwa dia tidak senang mengetahui secara tidak langsung atau tidak sama sekali.

options.text: The woman did not tell him about the ticket.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: Listen for Wishes and Untrue Conditions, the man's statement 'I wish you had told me about it' implies that the woman did NOT tell him about the ticket. Wishes about the past indicate the opposite of what happened.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: Listen for Wishes and Untrue Conditions, pernyataan pria itu 'Saya harap kamu memberitahuku tentang itu' menyiratkan bahwa wanita itu TIDAK memberitahunya tentang tiket tersebut. Harapan tentang masa lalu menunjukkan kebalikan dari apa yang terjadi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 14 - Listen for Wishes and Untrue Conditions

questions.passage:
(man) Did you see the work schedule for next week?
(woman) I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She is not working too many hours next week.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then,' which directly contradicts this option.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam saat itu,' yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan pilihan ini.

options.text: She doesn't have enough hours next week.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's statement is about having too many hours, not too few. The phrase 'so many hours' indicates an excess.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan wanita itu adalah tentang memiliki terlalu banyak jam kerja, bukan terlalu sedikit. Frasa 'begitu banyak jam' menunjukkan kelebihan.

options.text: She is working too many hours next week.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: LISTEN FOR CONTRARY MEANINGS (WISHES & UNTRUE CONDITIONS), the woman's statement 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then' implies the opposite is true. She is expressing a desire that is contrary to her current situation, meaning she IS working too many hours.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: LISTEN FOR CONTRARY MEANINGS (WISHES & UNTRUE CONDITIONS), pernyataan wanita 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam saat itu' menyiratkan kebalikannya yang benar. Dia mengungkapkan keinginan yang bertentangan dengan situasi saat ini, yang berarti dia BEKERJA terlalu banyak jam.

options.text: She likes working so much.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'I wish I didn't have to work' clearly indicates she does not like working so many hours, making this option the opposite of what she implies.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja' dengan jelas menunjukkan bahwa dia tidak suka bekerja begitu banyak jam, menjadikan pilihan ini kebalikan dari apa yang dia implikasikan.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 14: LISTEN FOR CONTRARY MEANINGS (WISHES & UNTRUE CONDITIONS)

questions.passage:
(man) Are you happy with the changes in the requirements for graduation?
(woman) I wish the department had not made the changes.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The department did not change the requirements.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states 'I wish the department had not made the changes,' indicating that the changes did occur.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya berharap departemen tidak membuat perubahan tersebut,' yang menunjukkan bahwa perubahan memang terjadi.

options.text: She likes the new requirements.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'I wish...' expresses a desire for something different from reality. In this case, she wishes the changes had NOT been made, meaning she does not like them.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'Saya berharap...' mengungkapkan keinginan untuk sesuatu yang berbeda dari kenyataan. Dalam kasus ini, dia berharap perubahan itu TIDAK dibuat, yang berarti dia tidak menyukainya.

options.text: She changed her apartment just before graduation.

reason.en: This is irrelevant. The dialogue is about changes in graduation requirements, not about changing an apartment.
reason.id: Ini tidak relevan. Dialog tersebut adalah tentang perubahan persyaratan kelulusan, bukan tentang mengubah apartemen.

options.text: She does not like the changes that the department made.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: LISTEN FOR WISHES AND UNTRUE CONDITIONS, the woman's statement 'I wish the department had not made the changes' implies the opposite of what she is saying. She is expressing regret about the changes, meaning she does not like them.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: LISTEN FOR WISHES AND UNTRUE CONDITIONS, pernyataan wanita 'Saya berharap departemen tidak membuat perubahan tersebut' menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang dia katakan. Dia mengungkapkan penyesalan tentang perubahan tersebut, yang berarti dia tidak menyukainya.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 14: LISTEN FOR WISHES AND UNTRUE CONDITIONS

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you going to the theater with us this weekend?
(man) I wish I had enough money to go.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY.

options.text: He is going to the theater.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he wishes he could go, implying he is not going. This answer contradicts his statement.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan keinginannya untuk pergi, menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak akan pergi. Jawaban ini bertentangan dengan pernyataannya.

options.text: He doesn't have enough money.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: LISTEN FOR CONTRARY MEANINGS (WISHES & UNTRUE CONDITIONS), the phrase 'I wish I had enough money' means the opposite is true, which is that he does not have enough money. This is the implied meaning.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: LISTEN FOR CONTRARY MEANINGS (WISHES & UNTRUE CONDITIONS), frasa 'I wish I had enough money' berarti kebalikannya yang benar, yaitu dia tidak punya cukup uang. Ini adalah makna tersirat.

options.text: He isn't afraid to go.

reason.en: The man's statement is about his financial situation, not his fear of going to the theater. This answer is irrelevant to the dialogue.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu berkaitan dengan situasi keuangannya, bukan ketakutannya untuk pergi ke teater. Jawaban ini tidak relevan dengan dialog.

options.text: He doesn't want to spend the money.

reason.en: While not having enough money prevents him from going, the primary implication of 'I wish I had enough money' is the lack of money itself, not a reluctance to spend it if he had it. This answer focuses on a secondary aspect.
reason.id: Meskipun tidak memiliki cukup uang mencegahnya pergi, implikasi utama dari 'I wish I had enough money' adalah kekurangan uang itu sendiri, bukan keengganan untuk membelanjakannya jika dia memilikinya. Jawaban ini berfokus pada aspek sekunder.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 14: LISTEN FOR CONTRARY MEANINGS (WISHES & UNTRUE CONDITIONS)

questions.passage:
(woman) Did you hear how Harry did on the astronomy exam?
(man) I bet he wishes he had studied harder.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Harry did not prepare enough for the exam.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14-15: Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions), the man's statement 'I bet he wishes he had studied harder' implies the opposite of what Harry actually did. Wishing he had studied harder means he did NOT study hard enough.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14-15: Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions), pernyataan pria 'I bet he wishes he had studied harder' menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang sebenarnya dilakukan Harry. Berharap dia belajar lebih giat berarti dia TIDAK belajar cukup giat.

options.text: Harry studied hard for the exam.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the man's statement 'I bet he wishes he had studied harder' directly contradicts this. Wishing he had studied harder implies he did not study hard.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena pernyataan pria 'I bet he wishes he had studied harder' secara langsung bertentangan dengan ini. Berharap dia belajar lebih giat menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak belajar giat.

options.text: He has not heard anything about Harry He had a bet with Harry.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The man is speculating about Harry's exam performance based on his wish, not stating he hasn't heard anything or that he had a bet with Harry. The phrase 'I bet' here is an idiom for strong belief or assumption, not a literal bet.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Pria tersebut berspekulasi tentang kinerja ujian Harry berdasarkan keinginannya, bukan menyatakan bahwa dia belum mendengar apa-apa atau bahwa dia bertaruh dengan Harry. Frasa 'I bet' di sini adalah idiom untuk keyakinan atau asumsi yang kuat, bukan taruhan harfiah.

options.text: The algebra course that she is taking is not her favorite.

reason.en: This option is irrelevant to the conversation. The dialogue is about Harry's astronomy exam, not about the man's or woman's algebra course.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak relevan dengan percakapan. Dialog tersebut adalah tentang ujian astronomi Harry, bukan tentang mata kuliah aljabar pria atau wanita tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skills 14-15 - Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions)

questions.passage:
(man) Do you have a good schedule of classes this semester?
(woman) I wish I didn't need to take this alge bra course. I'm not very good at math.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She doesn't need to take the algebra course.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: Listen for Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions), the woman says 'I wish I didn't need to take this algebra course.' This is a wish about the present, implying the opposite is true: she *does* need to take the course, but she wishes she didn't have to. Therefore, she implies she doesn't need to take it (but is forced to).
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: Listen for Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions), wanita itu berkata 'I wish I didn't need to take this algebra course.' Ini adalah harapan tentang masa kini, yang menyiratkan kebalikannya: dia *memang* perlu mengambil mata kuliah tersebut, tetapi dia berharap tidak perlu. Oleh karena itu, dia menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak perlu mengambilnya (tetapi terpaksa).

options.text: She has a good schedule of courses this semester.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'I wish I didn't need to take this algebra course' directly contradicts the idea that she has a good schedule. She is expressing dissatisfaction with one specific course.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'I wish I didn't need to take this algebra course' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia memiliki jadwal yang baik. Dia mengungkapkan ketidakpuasan dengan satu mata kuliah tertentu.

options.text: She's good at math, but she's taking the algebra course anyway.

reason.en: The woman states, 'I'm not very good at math.' This directly contradicts option [2], which claims she is good at math.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan, 'I'm not very good at math.' Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan opsi [2], yang mengklaim dia pandai matematika.

options.text: He was able to find a cheap apartment.

reason.en: The dialogue is about class schedules and the woman's ability in math. There is no mention of apartments or finances, making this option irrelevant.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas jadwal kuliah dan kemampuan wanita dalam matematika. Tidak ada penyebutan tentang apartemen atau keuangan, membuat opsi ini tidak relevan.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - Part A: Short Dialogues (Skills 1-17) - Skill 14: Listen for Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions)

questions.passage:
(woman) Your apartment is in a really great location!
(man) But I wish I had been able to find something cheaper.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: His apartment is too expensive.

reason.en: The man says 'I wish I had been able to find something cheaper,' which implies that he did NOT find something cheaper, and therefore his current apartment is too expensive for him.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata 'Saya berharap saya bisa menemukan sesuatu yang lebih murah,' yang menyiratkan bahwa dia TIDAK menemukan sesuatu yang lebih murah, dan oleh karena itu apartemennya saat ini terlalu mahal baginya.

options.text: He doesn't like the apartment's location.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man explicitly states that the apartment is in a 'really great location,' indicating he likes it. The 'wish' is about the price, not the location.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa apartemen itu berada di 'lokasi yang sangat bagus,' yang menunjukkan dia menyukainya. 'Harapan' itu tentang harga, bukan lokasi.

options.text: The apartment is cheap because of its location.

reason.en: This contradicts the man's statement. He wishes he had found something *cheaper*, implying his current apartment is *not* cheap, despite its good location.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu. Dia berharap dia menemukan sesuatu yang *lebih murah*, menyiratkan bahwa apartemennya saat ini *tidak* murah, meskipun lokasinya bagus.

options.text: He arrived early at the auditorium.

reason.en: This answer is completely irrelevant to the dialogue, which is about an apartment and its cost. There is no mention of arriving at an auditorium.
reason.id: Jawaban ini sama sekali tidak relevan dengan dialog, yang membahas tentang apartemen dan biayanya. Tidak ada penyebutan tentang kedatangan di auditorium.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 14 - Wishes and Untrue Conditions

questions.passage:
(woman) Why are you sitting all the way in the back of the auditorium?
(man) I wish I hadn't arrived so late. Then I could have gotten a better seat.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: He got one of the best seats in the auditorium.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the man's statement 'I wish I hadn't arrived so late' implies the opposite of getting a good seat. He is expressing regret about his late arrival, which prevented him from getting a better seat.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pernyataan pria 'Saya berharap saya tidak datang terlambat' menyiratkan kebalikan dari mendapatkan tempat duduk yang baik. Dia mengungkapkan penyesalan atas kedatangannya yang terlambat, yang mencegahnya mendapatkan tempat duduk yang lebih baik.

options.text: He was not early enough to get a seat at the front.

reason.en: This is the correct implication. The man says, 'I wish I hadn't arrived so late. Then I could have gotten a better seat.' This directly implies that because he arrived late, he was unable to get a seat at the front or a better one.
reason.id: Ini adalah implikasi yang benar. Pria itu berkata, 'Saya berharap saya tidak datang terlambat. Kalau begitu, saya bisa mendapatkan tempat duduk yang lebih baik.' Ini secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa karena dia datang terlambat, dia tidak dapat memperoleh tempat duduk di depan atau tempat duduk yang lebih baik.

options.text: He prefers sitting at the back.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement expresses regret about arriving late and not getting a better seat, which means he does not prefer sitting at the back. The phrase 'I wish I hadn't arrived so late' indicates he would have preferred to arrive earlier for a different outcome.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria itu mengungkapkan penyesalan karena datang terlambat dan tidak mendapatkan tempat duduk yang lebih baik, yang berarti dia tidak lebih suka duduk di belakang. Frasa 'Saya berharap saya tidak datang terlambat' menunjukkan bahwa dia lebih suka datang lebih awal untuk hasil yang berbeda.

options.text: He'd like to work on his social skills at the game.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue is about seating arrangements in an auditorium, not about attending a game or social skills. The man's wish is related to his seating preference due to his arrival time.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tersebut adalah tentang pengaturan tempat duduk di auditorium, bukan tentang menghadiri pertandingan atau keterampilan sosial. Harapan pria itu berkaitan dengan preferensi tempat duduknya karena waktu kedatangannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skills 1–17) - Skill 14: Listen for Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions)

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you going to the football game this weekend?
(man) I wish I could, but I have to work on my sociology paper.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He wishes he could work on his term paper for sociology.

reason.en: The man wishes he could go to the game, not that he could work on his paper. The phrase 'work on my sociology paper' is the reason he cannot go.
reason.id: Pria itu berharap dia bisa pergi ke pertandingan, bukan berharap dia bisa mengerjakan makalahnya. Frasa 'mengerjakan makalah sosiologi saya' adalah alasan mengapa dia tidak bisa pergi.

options.text: He can't attend the game because of his schoolwork.

reason.en: This is a plausible inference, but it misses the nuance of the 'wish' statement, which implies a desire contrary to fact. The man doesn't just 'have to work'; he 'wishes he could' do something else.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesimpulan yang masuk akal, tetapi tidak menangkap nuansa pernyataan 'wish' (berharap), yang menyiratkan keinginan yang bertentangan dengan kenyataan. Pria itu tidak hanya 'harus bekerja'; dia 'berharap dia bisa' melakukan hal lain.

options.text: Sociology is less important to him than football this weekend.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: Listen for Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions), the statement 'I wish I could' indicates that the opposite is true. He wishes he could go to the game, implying that his schoolwork (sociology paper) is preventing him, and therefore, football is less important to him than his schoolwork in this specific situation.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: Listen for Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions), pernyataan 'I wish I could' (Saya berharap saya bisa) menunjukkan bahwa kebalikannya yang benar. Dia berharap dia bisa pergi ke pertandingan, menyiratkan bahwa pekerjaan sekolahnya (makalah sosiologi) menghalanginya, dan oleh karena itu, sepak bola kurang penting baginya daripada pekerjaan sekolahnya dalam situasi spesifik ini.

reason.en: This option is incomplete and cannot be evaluated.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak lengkap dan tidak dapat dievaluasi.

parts.title: SKILL 15: LISTEN FOR UNTRUE CONDITIONS
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of untrue conditions.

questions.passage:
(woman) I'm glad I called and told you about the meeting.
(man) If you had not called me, I would not have known.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The woman did not need to call him.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the man explicitly states that if the woman had NOT called, he would not have known. This implies her call was necessary.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa jika wanita itu TIDAK menelepon, dia tidak akan tahu. Ini menyiratkan bahwa panggilannya diperlukan.

options.text: The woman called to let him know about the meeting.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 10: Listen for Negatives with Comparatives, the man's statement 'If you had not called me, I would not have known' is a conditional statement that, when analyzed, means the opposite is true. The structure implies that because she *did* call, he *did* know. The woman's first line confirms she called to inform him about the meeting.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 10: Listen for Negatives with Comparatives, pernyataan pria 'If you had not called me, I would not have known' adalah pernyataan bersyarat yang, ketika dianalisis, berarti kebalikannya yang benar. Strukturnya menyiratkan bahwa karena dia *memang* menelepon, dia *memang* tahu. Baris pertama wanita itu mengonfirmasi bahwa dia menelepon untuk memberitahunya tentang pertemuan tersebut.

options.text: He's not glad that the woman called.

reason.en: This contradicts the man's statement. He implies the call was beneficial by saying he would not have known without it.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu. Dia menyiratkan bahwa panggilan itu bermanfaat dengan mengatakan bahwa dia tidak akan tahu tanpanya.

options.text: He already knew about the meeting when the woman called.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement 'If you had not called me, I would not have known' directly implies that he did *not* know before she called.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria itu 'If you had not called me, I would not have known' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia *tidak* tahu sebelum wanita itu menelepon.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 10: Listen for Negatives with Comparatives

questions.passage:
(man) I always seem to get stopped by the police.
(woman) If you drove more slowly, the police would not stop you so often.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man often drives too quickly.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the woman's statement 'If you drove more slowly, the police would not stop you so often' implies that the man's current driving habit is the opposite of driving slowly, meaning he often drives too quickly. This is a correct inference from the conditional statement.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan wanita 'If you drove more slowly, the police would not stop you so often' menyiratkan bahwa kebiasaan mengemudi pria saat ini adalah kebalikan dari mengemudi perlahan, yang berarti dia sering mengemudi terlalu cepat. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang benar dari pernyataan kondisional tersebut.

options.text: The police do not stop the man too much.

reason.en: This option directly contradicts the woman's statement. The woman implies the man is stopped often because he drives too fast, not that the police do not stop him too much.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita. Wanita itu menyiratkan bahwa pria itu sering dihentikan karena dia mengemudi terlalu cepat, bukan karena polisi tidak terlalu sering menghentikannya.

options.text: The man drove rather slowly.
